seed: safety vs exposure; pressure_vs_release; split_vertical; echo_right; braid against ripple; pressure_border; thin_vs_heavy; vessel; softness_severity | abstract variation request 353; target batch count 500
title: Echo of Protection
reasoning: The emotional tone balances the juxtaposition of safety and exposure, creating a visual dialogue between warmth and vulnerability. The chosen palette of soft greens and pale skin tones evoke feelings of shelter, contrasted by cool whites and blues signifying exposure. The main collision occurs at the center, where 'safety' colors crowd around a more open, inviting expanse of 'exposure', pushing against the borders to create tension. The layout suggests a pulse—two small centers vying for space across negative areas, which embodies the pattern of 'teeth' against 'flood', demonstrating struggle and softness. The inclusion of broken pixels introduces imperfection and resilience, enhancing the theme of fighting against exposure. Negative space allows for breathing room, ensuring the composition feels intentional yet spontaneous, reflecting the dynamics of safety and risk.

layout.name: two_pulses
layout.description: Two small centers pulse toward or away from each other across negative space.

composition.name: exposure_vs_shelter
composition.idea: A pale exposed center is either protected by a frame or left open to incoming marks.

referenceExample.title: Pride Falling Into Shame
referenceExample.pattern: crown shape dissolving into dark stair steps

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
